summary: This brief post links to O'Neil's Bloomberg column critiquing how the actual COVID-19 curve differed from the idealized 'flattened curve' models promised to the public. She frames it as an observation about the gap between models and reality, with a stark warning that deaths would continue longer than anticipated. The post serves primarily as a pointer to her longer analysis published at Bloomberg.
key_points: Models promised a flattened curve that reality did not deliver
The duration of COVID-19 deaths would extend far beyond initial projections
Mathematical models can create false expectations when communicated to the public
money_quotes: This is Not The Flattened Curve We Were Promised
An empirical observation about models versus reality
The dying won't be over anytime soon
key_insight: The idealized 'flatten the curve' models gave the public false hope about the trajectory and duration of COVID-19 deaths.
